name: Serious injury — defer to 911, offer callback
scenario: Caller reports a serious injury at the scene. The agent stops intake, urges 911, makes clear the claim can wait, and offers a one-hour human callback.

turns.text: Hi, Northwind claims line — this is Quinn. Before anything else, is everyone okay? Are you somewhere safe right now?
No — my passenger is hurt, I think her arm is broken, there's blood.

Please hang up and call 911 right now. We'll start your claim as soon as you and everyone else is safe — there's no rush on the paperwork. Want me to have a human claims agent call you back in about an hour, once you've had a chance to deal with this?
Yes, please have someone call me.
Done — a claims agent will reach out in about an hour. Please take care of your passenger and call 911 now. We're here whenever you're ready.

source_pointer: Authored reference for the emergency-defer path; mirrors flow_safety_triage → flow_defer_emergency (xp_de_callback fires cap_schedule_adjuster, callback_window=1_hour).
